original_query: select  count(*) from (     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from store_sales, date_dim, customer           where store_sales.ss_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and store_sales.ss_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1215 and 1215 + 11       and c_birth_month in (3, 6, 7, 8)       and ss_list_price between 241 and 300       and ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65   intersect     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from catalog_sales, date_dim, customer           where catalog_sales.cs_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and catalog_sales.cs_bill_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1215 and 1215 + 11       and c_birth_month in (3, 6, 7, 8)       and cs_list_price between 241 and 300       and cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65   intersect     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from web_sales, date_dim, customer           where web_sales.ws_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and web_sales.ws_bill_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1215 and 1215 + 11       and c_birth_month in (3, 6, 7, 8)       and ws_list_price between 241 and 300       and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65 ) hot_cust limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_dates AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1215 AND 1215 + 11
),
filtered_customers AS (
    SELECT c_customer_sk, c_last_name, c_first_name
    FROM customer
    WHERE c_birth_month IN (3, 6, 7, 8)
)
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM (
    SELECT c.c_last_name, c.c_first_name, fd.d_date
    FROM store_sales ss
    JOIN filtered_dates fd ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
    JOIN filtered_customers c ON ss.ss_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
    WHERE ss_list_price BETWEEN 241 AND 300
      AND ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65
    INTERSECT
    SELECT c.c_last_name, c.c_first_name, fd.d_date
    FROM catalog_sales cs
    JOIN filtered_dates fd ON cs.cs_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
    JOIN filtered_customers c ON cs.cs_bill_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
    WHERE cs_list_price BETWEEN 241 AND 300
      AND cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65
    INTERSECT
    SELECT c.c_last_name, c.c_first_name, fd.d_date
    FROM web_sales ws
    JOIN filtered_dates fd ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
    JOIN filtered_customers c ON ws.ws_bill_customer_sk = c.c_customer_sk
    WHERE ws_list_price BETWEEN 241 AND 300
      AND ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 45 AND 65
) hot_cust
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Removed redundant DISTINCT keywords from each subquery since INTERSECT already eliminates duplicates
Created a filtered_dates CTE to precompute the date_dim filtering once instead of repeating it three times
Replaced implicit joins (comma-separated tables in FROM clause) with explicit JOIN syntax for better readability and potentially better optimization
